steps.title: Identify the vertex from the problem
steps.content:
The problem states that 2 seconds after launch, the object reaches a maximum height of 203 feet. This is the vertex of the parabola:
  • Time at maximum: $h = 2$
  • Maximum height: $k = 203$
  • Vertex: $(2, 203)$

steps.title: Apply vertex form of a parabola
steps.content:
The vertex form of a parabola is $f(t) = a(t - h)^2 + k$, where $(h, k)$ is the vertex. Substituting $h = 2$ and $k = 203$:
$$f(t) = a(t - 2)^2 + 203$$
Now I need to find $a$.

steps.title: Find the coefficient using the initial condition
steps.content:
The object is launched from 139 feet above the ground, so $f(0) = 139$:
$$139 = a(0 - 2)^2 + 203$$
$$139 = 4a + 203$$
$$4a = -64$$
$$a = -16$$
Therefore, $f(t) = -16(t - 2)^2 + 203$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
